meta.name: Potato Leek Soup
meta.subtitle: Creamy classic (or dairy-free) • straightforward, cozy bowl

source.notes: SSA baseline: simple ingredient list, optional dairy-free finish, freezer-friendly (best frozen without dairy).

recommended: fine mesh strainer (optional for silky texture)

tasteProfile.notes: Soft, creamy potato base with sweet onion-leek aroma. Finish with cream or dairy-free option.

ingredients.label: Butter or olive oil
ingredients.notes: About 2 tablespoons. Butter adds classic richness.

ingredients.label: Leeks, cleaned and sliced (white + light green parts)
ingredients.notes: About 3 medium leeks. Clean thoroughly—sand hides between layers.

ingredients.label: Yellow onion, diced (optional)
ingredients.notes: Adds depth if leeks are small or mild.

ingredients.label: Potatoes, peeled and diced
ingredients.notes: Yukon Gold for buttery texture; Russet for extra creamy body.

ingredients.label: Vegetable broth (or chicken broth if not strict vegetarian), low-sodium preferred
Water (optional, to adjust thickness)
ingredients.notes: Add as needed during simmer or after blending.

ingredients.label: Fresh thyme sprigs or dried thyme (optional)
Salt
ingredients.notes: Start with ~1 teaspoon; adjust at end based on broth.

ingredients.label: Whole milk (optional lighter finish)
ingredients.notes: About 3/4–1 cup. Warm gently; avoid hard boil.

ingredients.label: Unsweetened coconut milk (optional dairy-free finish)
ingredients.notes: About 2/3 cup. Choose unsweetened; adds subtle sweetness.

ingredients.label: Extra-virgin olive oil (optional dairy-free richness)
ingredients.notes: Drizzle in after blending if not using coconut milk.

ingredients.label: Chives or green onions, sliced (optional for serving)

steps.title: Prep and soften leeks
instructions: Clean leeks thoroughly: slice lengthwise, rinse between layers, then slice into half-moons.
Melt butter (or heat oil) in a pot over medium heat.
Add leeks (and onion if using). Cook 10–12 minutes, stirring, until soft and sweet but not browned.
Add garlic (if using) and cook 30 seconds.

steps.title: Simmer potatoes until tender
instructions: Add diced potatoes, broth, bay leaf (optional), thyme (optional), salt, and pepper.
Bring to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat and simmer 18–25 minutes until potatoes are very tender.

steps.title: Blend to desired texture
instructions: Remove bay leaf and thyme sprigs if used.
Blend with an immersion blender until smooth (or leave slightly chunky if you prefer).
If using a countertop blender, blend in batches and vent the lid carefully.

steps.title: Finish: creamy or dairy-free
instructions: • Creamy: stir in heavy cream OR milk and warm gently (do not hard-boil).
• Dairy-free: stir in coconut milk OR drizzle in extra-virgin olive oil for richness.
Adjust thickness with a little water or broth if needed.
Taste and adjust salt/pepper; add a small squeeze of lemon juice (optional) for brightness.

steps.title: Serve
instructions: Serve hot. Garnish with chives/green onions if desired.
Pairs well with crusty bread, biscuits, or grilled cheese.

bestPractices: Freeze best before adding dairy (cream/milk). Add dairy when reheating for best texture.
Cool quickly; portion into flat freezer bags or containers.

useWhatYouHaveLogic.notes: If no leeks: substitute onion (300g) + a pinch of celery seed (optional). If no potatoes: use cauliflower (different texture) or add white beans for body.

storage.fridge: Refrigerate up to 5 days. Soup thickens when chilled; loosen with broth/water on reheat.
storage.freezer: Freeze up to 3 months (prefer freezing without dairy).
storage.reheat: Reheat gently to a simmer, stirring often; avoid boiling hard after adding dairy.

notes: Do not brown the leeks—keeping them soft preserves the classic sweet flavor.
For an extra-silky soup, strain after blending (optional) or blend longer.
If using fresh milled flour bread alongside this soup, toast slices for structure and a better dunk.

audit.testNotes: Schema-compliant: schemaVersion+meta present; ingredients include id+label+amount; steps include id+title+instructions. No disallowed 'toppings' property used.
